definition_core: Stat describing how quickly an element can move. Two basic movement types (Cautious and Patrol) are based on M value. If reduced to 0, the element is Immobilised. Can be restored via Repair or Self-Repair.

Represents a mix of an element's volume of fire, range, and accuracy. F is the base number of dice rolled when shooting. If reduced to 0, the element is Disarmed. Can be restored via Reload, Repair, or Self-Repair.

Represents an element's countermeasures, electronics, and ability to recover from enemy action. D is the number of dice rolled for Defence rolls against shooting. If reduced to 0, the element is Deactivated.

Represents the durability of an element's passive defence. Enemies add the target's A to their effective range when shooting. If reduced to 0, the element is Destroyed. Cannot be restored by standard repair unless Regenerative Armour is equipped.

Represents the size or threat class of an element (C1 Light, C2 Medium, C3 Heavy, C4 Siege/Assault). Dictates cost to include in a battlegroup, Force Class contribution, and effectiveness in Close Combat.
The maximum total Class points each player may spend on their battlegroup. Commanders agree on an equal FC before mustering. Typical values: 15 FC (~45 min), 20 FC (~1 hr), 30 FC (~3 hrs).
Extra funds spent on Assets before a mission. For every 5 Class of a detachment, the commander receives 1 BP (rounded down). Unspent BP can be converted to Victory Points if victorious.
Scored when opposing elements are destroyed or disabled, usually 1 VP per 1C of the destroyed element. Used to determine the winner at the end of a mission or campaign.
A clear visual path between an element and its target. Blocked by Solid terrain, and obscured by Foliage and Smoke. A total Cover value of 6+ means the target is outside LOS.
The path along which an element can direct its weapons at a target. Determined by the element's Arc of Fire. Must be clear for shooting actions to be valid.
The field of awareness for an element. Determines whether it can detect and react to enemy actions. Cautious movement grants 360-degree AoV; Patrol retains the standard arc.

The field in which an element can direct its weapons. Omnidirectional Aircraft have 180-degree AoF; Fixed-Wing have 90-degree frontal. Alert elements or those with Enclosed Turret Array can achieve 360 degrees.

An artificially determined zone spanning from the ground up to 12 inches above the tallest terrain feature. This is the operational area for Hardwar aircraft. Elements leaving the LAZ are considered retreating.
definition_quickplay: All Aircraft are at 6-inch height at all times unless landed. LAZ and Elevation Change rules are ignored.

definition_core: Tracks overall deterioration of an element due to damage. Filled by the same amount each time a stat is reduced. Cannot be restored in any way. Also indicates difficulty level for repairing active stats.
definition_quickplay: The Damage Bar is equal to twice the element's C (e.g., C1 = 2, C4 = 8).

definition_core: A movement action allowing an element to travel across the battlefield. Elements choose Cautious (up to M value, 360-degree AoV), Patrol (up to 2xM), or Rapid (up to 3xM, if available) modes.

A ranged attack action. Declare target in LoF, measure range (add target's A and Cover for effective range), roll F dice for Attack Roll, target rolls D dice for Defence Roll, then calculate hits by grouping dice to meet or exceed effective range.
Costs 2 Action Tokens (entire activation). Adds +1F to the attack. Enemy elements may react before the aimed attack resolves.

Can be performed in either order. Element performs a Cautious or Patrol move, but F is halved (rounded up). Target must be declared visible before moving and must be in LoS/LoF at the moment of shooting.

Can only be performed as the last action during activation. The element saves its second action as a reaction to be used later in the turn. A Guard Token is placed; removed after the first reaction or at end of turn.

Initiates Close Combat. Target must be in LoS, within reach, and path must be a straight line. Costs 1 Action Token. A Combat Roll (based on C) is performed with modifiers for distance, motive type, and special rules.

A special action creating a Dangerous Terrain zone. Roll basic F to set max distance, place a marker; the area within F-inch radius becomes Dangerous. Elements in the zone are pinned. Lasts until end of turn or a successful attack on the suppressing element. Costs 1F permanently (restorable via Reload).

Similar to Suppressive Fire but only for Indirect Fire weapons. Roll basic F for max distance; place marker; area within F-inch radius becomes Dangerous Terrain. LoS is not required. Costs 1F permanently (restorable via Reload).

Any element can transmit coordinates of opposing forces to Indirect Fire elements. Target must be in LoS. Costs 1 Action Token. Token placed on target; all Indirect Fire units may aim at the marked element. Removed after first attack or end of turn.
definition_quickplay: If the calling element has LoS to the target, no Action Token is spent -- effectively free.

definition_core: A special action to restore ammo or energy capacitors. Roll dice equal to element's D stat; if at least one die exceeds the total Damage Bar tally, the Reload succeeds and restores all abilities requiring reloading.
The element performs no action; remove a token as normal. Opposing elements may still react if they can draw LoS. Reserve elements performing Do Nothing cannot trigger reactions.
A special action to board a Transport Bay or Troop Transport. The embarking element must be in base contact and at the same elevation as its transport. Embarking ends the action.

A special action to exit a Transport Bay or Troop Transport. Elements are placed in base contact with the carrier at the same height. Aircraft passengers may only disembark if they can safely touch the ground.

Available to Aircraft with Transport capacity. One element per action can be dropped. The dropped element takes a Crash test with all damage halved and rounded down. Height of drop is the Crash test basis.

Element moves up to its M value in inches, maintaining 360-degree situational awareness. Grants the Alert special rule (360-degree AoV) for the following action during the same activation, including Guard.
Default movement mode. The element moves up to 2x its M value, retaining its standard Arc of Vision. Moving quickly forward, ready to engage targets of opportunity.

The element moves up to 3x its M value, sacrificing situational awareness for speed. Only one facing change allowed, at beginning or end of movement. Opposing elements treated as impassable. Requires the Rapid special rule or ability.
Available only to Walkers. Allows the element to duck/squat/kneel to reduce its silhouette. Can only move at Cautious rate while ducking. Check LoS as high as its pelvis, ignoring the upper half.
definition_quickplay: This action cannot be used in Quickplay rules.

definition_core: Covers all wheeled vehicles (bikes, trikes, APCs, etc.). On Paved terrain, gains the Rapid special rule even without it normally. Can only be taken by Vehicle and Trooper elements.
Mainstay of armed forces. Ignores Rough terrain and treats Difficult terrain as Rough for mobility purposes. Can only be taken by Vehicle elements.
Biped, quadruped, or multi-legged elements (Hardbots, Labots). Can perform Duck & Move manoeuvre and scale cliff obstacles no taller than 1 inch. Can only be taken by Walker elements.

Craft that move by sliding over the ground or water (hoverbikes, speeders, anti-grav skiffs). Treat Forests and Ruins as Dangerous but ignore Paved, Rough, and Difficult terrain while airborne. Receive -1 C in Charge resolution.
Includes spinners, jetpacks, helicopters, drones, anti-grav, and VTOLs. Can hover, move sideways, stop mid-air. When airborne, cannot Do Nothing; must Cautious Move instead. 180/180-degree AoF/AoV.

All planes (gliders, jets, propeller). Cannot perform Cautious movement and must perform at least one Move action per turn or stall. Can land only in Rapid mode. 90-degree frontal AoF, 360-degree AoV. Aircraft only.
Infantry-type motive covering soldiers, cyborgs, humanoid robots, biped hardsuits, and drones. Can scale Cliffs and Building walls at Cautious speed, ignore Rough Terrain, and enter Buildings.
Applies to trains, monorails, maglevs, and tethered hover trains. May only move along Rails, which they treat as Paved. A Rail element forced off its track is Immobilised.

Allows fast travel. All ground elements gain +1 M when traveling entirely on Paved terrain. Rapid elements get one additional turn during movement. Wheeled elements gain Rapid ability. Troopers may only Dig In when in base contact with non-Paved terrain.
Default terrain type. Usually flat; hills can share Plain characteristics. No specific movement rules apply. Represented by permafrost, glacier, savannah, fields, meadows, plateaus, beaches, etc.
Elements may move at any rate but M is reduced by -1 during a Move action passing through Rough terrain. Tracked vehicles treat Rough as Plain. Hovercraft and Aircraft ignore it while airborne.

M reduced by -1. If moving at Rapid rate, roll D12 vs distance moved; fail causes a non-critical hit. Move & Shoot actions cannot be performed through Difficult terrain. Tracked vehicles treat as Rough.
M reduced by -1. At Patrol rate, roll D12 vs distance; fail means a non-critical hit. At Rapid rate, automatic hit plus D12 check for a second. Tracked vehicles treat as Difficult.

When moving in, out, or through, roll D12; if result is less than total distance moved, the element suffers a non-critical hit. May be an additional feature of any other terrain type, stacking with their rules.
Shares Dangerous terrain features, but instead of dealing damage on a failed D12 roll, the element loses 1 action this turn.
Elements may not enter Impassable terrain. If forced to enter, they are destroyed. Represented by lava rivers, glacier cracks, ravines, cliff sides, fortress walls, etc.

Terrain undulations with gentle sides, traversable without trouble if a based mini can be placed stably. If too steep, treated as Cliff. Hills can combine Plain, Rough, Difficult, or Dangerous characteristics.

Steep slopes, escarpments, fortification walls, buildings. Scaled at Cautious speed by Troopers and Walkers with Grasping Manipulators. Features no taller than 1 inch traversable by regular Walkers. All others treat as Impassable.
Terrain that completely blocks Line of Sight. Elements cannot see or shoot through Solid terrain. Represented by intact buildings, concrete walls, and other opaque obstacles.
See-through terrain that partially blocks LoS, adding +1 to effective range per 1 inch of terrain between attacker and target. Represented by ruins, malls, hangars, and skeletal buildings.
See-through terrain adding +1 to effective range per 1 inch between attacker and target. Does not provide Partial Cover. Found in forests, scrub, and hedges.
Increases effective range by +2 inches per 1 inch of LoS covered. 3 inches of Smoke blocks LoS completely. Dissipates after one turn unless sustained. Also covers dust, vapour, and fire.

Rolls of 11 and 12 become Exploding Dice during tests. Laser and Plasma are -1F against submerged targets. Flamethrowers have no effect on submerged elements. Partially submerged elements are in partial cover.
Receives only half the hits from each attack, rounded down. Critical hit clusters are not halved. Does not suffer damage from supporting elements on top of it.
Usually buildings in off-world environments (Moon, Mars). Destroyed when Breached but do not turn into Ruins until Armour reaches 0. Everything inside is destroyed when Breached.
Fortified/Pressurised buildings are Breached after losing half their Armour. Structural integrity is open for enemy Troopers to infiltrate. Non-building Fortified terrain cannot be Breached.
Susceptible to fire damage from Laser, Plasma, and Flamer weapons. Burns at 1 damage per turn. Burning terrain becomes Dangerous and covered in Smoke.

Fuel tanks, pipelines, reactors, ammo warehouses. When breached, destroys everything on/within and creates a blast with range and F equal to original A value, reducing by 1 per inch. D rolls cannot defend against explosions.
Element with M reduced to 0. Cannot move or change facing but can still react and perform non-movement actions. If both Disarmed and Immobilised at end of game, considered Destroyed for VP.
Element with F reduced to 0. Cannot attack or use F-stat actions but can perform other actions and react. If both Disarmed and Immobilised at end of game, considered Destroyed for VP.
Element with D reduced to 0. Cannot perform any action type and, unless repaired, is considered Destroyed for Victory Points purposes.
Element with A reduced to 0. Becomes a Wreck if Damage Bar points remain. Grants VP to opponent equal to its Class value.

Element that leaves the battlefield outside mission objectives. Cannot re-enter. Grants half C (rounded up) in VP to opponent. The retreating side receives half C (rounded down) in VP in survivability scenarios.
Receives one fewer Action Token per turn until recovered. Not immobilized but cannot perform movement actions until getting up.
Commander with jammed comms cannot call in Reserves or Strategic Assets, and the battleforce cannot use special actions for the turn.
definition_quickplay: Electronic warfare uses Interference Token dice pools instead of frequency guessing.

definition_core: Element holding a Guard Token after performing a Guard action. May react once to any opposing element's action within LoS. Reactions include Move, Shoot, Move & Shoot, Counter-Charge, Castle, or Do Nothing.
Elements within a Suppressive Fire or Area Bombardment zone. Must undergo a Dangerous Terrain test for each action other than Do Nothing while remaining in the zone.
State after using Full Strike -- energy capacitors drained, barrels overheated, ammo depleted. F reduced by 2 (not on Damage Bar). Requires Reload before Full Strike can be used again.
A passive defender in Close Combat (out of Action Tokens, not in Guard mode). Deals no damage unless Critical Hits are applied.
Disabled elements are automatically destroyed if charged (no Combat Roll). Non-Trooper elements disabled during Close Combat are considered Destroyed even if winning.

In solo/co-op, AI elements detected during spawning (rolling 11+ on D dice) are placed here. They get a sneak attack from within 8 inches of the triggering element, then spawn into play at the start of the next turn.
After each player action, roll D12 plus current turn number. On 8+, an enemy spawns. On 1-7, nothing happens. Spawned type determined by Spawn Type Tables (C1-C4 elements or Strategic Asset).

Determines which player element an AI element targets. Order: 1) element that triggered spawn, 2) element closest to objective, 3) element with highest C. Tiebreaker: C3-4 AI targets highest C in LoS; C1-2 targets lowest.
In solo/co-op, rolling 11-12 on the Spawn Table generates an AI Strategic Asset. Roll D12 for results including Airstrikes, Artillery Strikes, Sabotage, Repair Team, or Reinforcements.

Tokens for electronic warfare offense/defense. Received from CHQ (1), RHQ (2), NHQ (2), plus 1 per Hacker. Used to fortify own comms or jam opponent's. In solo/co-op, AI gets only 5 total for the mission.
definition_quickplay: Uses dice pool opposition instead of frequency guessing. Matching dice cancel each other.

definition_core: Solo/co-op military infrastructure (HQ, comms, logistics). Deployed in Open Terrain. Captured when AI element ends action within 3 inches. Worth C2 in VP if captured. Three types: Frontline HQ, Logistics Hub, Comms Node.
Field Asset granting +1 BP for HQ Assets. If captured by AI, the AI immediately rolls on the Strategic Assets AI Spawn Table.
Field Asset granting +1 C to spend towards FC. If captured by AI, the AI immediately rolls on the Strategic Assets AI Spawn Table.
Field Asset granting +2 Interference Tokens. If captured, player loses 2 Interference Tokens; if unable, AI rolls on Strategic Assets AI Spawn Table.
Available to Aircraft and Troopers. Element always treated as having Deep Deployment from Reserves. Aircraft do not have to land after entering. Troopers must land.

Tactical asset (3 BP). Only if commander wins Initiative. Up to 3 elements (total C no higher than 6) placed anywhere after opponent deploys. Must be Cx2 inches from nearest enemy with terrain Cover. Must Reload to activate.
definition_quickplay: Can only be used if commander wins Initiative via Quickplay method (lower Action Tokens).

definition_core: Special action granting +3 Cover bonus as long as element remains in place. Lasts until the element performs a Move or Move & Shoot action.
Element always has 360-degree Arc of Vision (AoV). Also granted temporarily to any element performing a Cautious move for its following action.
Element in base contact with damaged element rolls D dice; for each die exceeding total Damage Bar, restore 1 point of M, F, or D (not Armour). Damage Bar is not reduced.
Unleashes all weapons in one attack. Gains +2F but afterwards F permanently reduced by 2 (not on Damage Bar). Cannot reuse until Reload restores F.
Two or more barrels/synchronized fire mode. Allows a re-roll of one Attack die per shot.
Flaw: element is slow to react and cannot perform the Guard action. Cannot be taken by elements with Alert.
Deploy as an action to remove all Relay Coordinates markers from the element.
AA attack does not add height difference when calculating distance to target Aircraft. Still useful against ground targets.
Attack Roll die becomes an Augment Die on rolls of 11 and 12, providing greater chance of extra damage.
Powerful armor-piercing weapon. If attack deals at least 1 damage, the first damage point is a critical hit (attacker chooses stat).
If the attack deals at least 1 damage, causes an additional +1 non-critical hit.
Removes one remaining action from target (including Guard) in addition to normal damage. If attack fails on range, it jumps to closest element (friend or foe) within rolled range.
Successful hit causes no damage but shuts down target until next turn. Removes all Action Tokens and ends continuous actions (Guard, Suppressive Fire, etc.).

Auto-hits all targets in a 1-inch wide, F-inch long corridor. Uses half F dice. Distance treated as 0 (only Armour counts). Ignores Cover and Dig In. Double damage vs Troopers. Must Reload after each use.
Rapid-fire rotary cannons or pulse lasers. Reduces the Armour distance modifier to target by -2.
Smart ammunition attacks through cover with -2 Cover modifier reduction. Maximum Cover LoS rule (6+) still applies.
Attacks without LoS. Full F with active Spotter, -1F with Relay Coordinates, half F with no spotting. Range from firing unit. Can fire from Reserves.
Attacks without LoS via friendly Spotter coordinates. Range measured from Spotter, not firing unit. Can fire from Reserves.
Anti-personnel weapons (flechette guns, needlers, metal storm, scatter guns). Deals double damage to Troopers.
One successful hit causes -1 each to M, F, A (3 damage on track). Additional/critical hits discarded. Comes with free Complex Reload flaw.
Walker only. Ignores Suppressive Fire. Reduces opponent's C by 1 in Charge/Close Combat. +1C when used in Block & Parry.
Sensor array for painting targets. Spend 1 Action Token; all targets in LoS painted for Guided/Indirect Fire allies. Guided Fire measures range from Spotter. Removed after firing or end of turn.
Roll D dice equal to element's D; for each die exceeding Damage Bar total, restore 1 point of M, F, or D (not Armour). Damage Bar unchanged.
Use as an action. Until end of turn, element's Cover increased by +3 in addition to other cover bonuses.
Blades, axes, or claws adding +1 C during Charge and Close Combat Hit actions.
Dozer blade or ram granting +1C to Combat Roll during Charge. Vehicles only.
Walker only. Enables Climb, Flip, Grab & Hold, Intercept & Pull, Lift & Throw, and Pick & Carry/Drag special actions.
Move in a straight line ignoring terrain no taller than M value. Cannot Move & Shoot. Triggers reactions from brief LoS exposure. Exhausted after use; requires Reload. Walkers, Troopers, Hovercraft.
Adds +2 Cover. Only +4 from terrain needed to break LoS. First successful hit disables the camouflage.
Troopers and Aircraft only. Element does not trigger Guard responses when activated.
In addition to normal damage, target cannot perform special actions until end of turn (as if jammed comms).
Walker-size machine gun. Reduces target's D by 1 for attack resolution.
Ignores Partial Cover entirely. Ignores the 6+ cover threshold, allowing fire through cover (other cover types still apply).
Graviton laser / shockwave cannon. Successful hit causes target to Crash from elevation 1. Each additional hit adds 1 to Crash height.
In addition to Charge/CC damage, removes one remaining action from target and cancels standing orders (Guard, Spotter).
Close combat weapon (plasma cutters, electrified maces, monofilament blades). CC Roll dice become Augment Dice on 11 and 12.
Resolves like standard cannon. On hit, may sacrifice a hit to grapple target (ranged CC lock). Can sacrifice second hit for Grasping Manipulator actions.
180-degree front arc force-field spanning F inches. Attacks through it have F halved (rounded down); F1 bounces off. Activated/sustained by Shoot or Move & Shoot.
Activated by Shoot/Move & Shoot. Enemy cannot trace LoF unless more elements than cloaked unit's F can see it. Must be sustained each turn.
Creates quantum field in 3-inch frontal arc providing F worth of Cover (half if Move & Shoot) to all elements on both sides. Removes Spotter markers. Sustained by Shoot/Move & Shoot.
All damage applied to D stat only. Excess hits lost. Only critical hits may target other stats.
Close-range weapon. No Augmented Dice, no Critical Hits. Each scored hit is doubled instead.
Enables Assault Hack (remove Action Tokens), Control Override (take over enemy), Duel (cyberspace combat), Power/Sensory/Viral Overload (reduce stats).
Allows embarking elements up to C minus 1. Can take twice for double capacity. Can carry Disposable Swarm Drones.
+1 D in Defence rolls against incoming fire, +1 C during Charge/Close Combat.
Structural regeneration (nanocrystal, ceramite, carbotitanium). May Repair or Self-Repair Armour stat in addition to other active stats.
Incendiary weapon. First hit sets target ablaze; subsequent hits standard. Burning element treats all targets as behind Smokescreen (+3 Cover). Removable by Repair/Self-Repair.
Non-lethal. On hit, target's M halved (rounded down). Removable by Repair/Self-Repair.
No damage, but on hit each Move action treated as Dangerous terrain due to armour-slicing web entanglement. Removable by Repair/Self-Repair.
Occurs when Attack/Combat Roll produces twin pair not cancelled by D roll. Attacking commander chooses which stat to reduce.
definition_quickplay: Each twin pair causes additional 1 damage, stacking per pair in same group.

definition_core: When Attack/Combat Roll produces a 12, an additional die is rolled and added. Can chain further Augment Dice. Defence rolls also benefit.
May spend action or discard Guard token to add own F dice to D roll against Guided/Indirect Fire. Can add half F (rounded up) to nearby friendly element's D roll within 6 inches.
May move at Rapid speed in Difficult terrain without penalties and re-rolls dice for crossing Hazardous terrain.
May re-roll a single D die after the Attacker's F roll. Second roll replaces first even if worse.
May re-roll a single F die after the target's D roll. Second roll replaces first even if worse.
In Shoot or Move & Shoot, may split F between two targets and roll separately. Can be taken multiple times for additional targets.
Scan special action pinpoints enemy elements within Fx12 inches, ignoring all obstacles. Effects last until end of turn for Relay Coordinates or Spotter actions.
Once per game, spend an action to turn Plain/Paved/Hill terrain up to 3x3 inches into Dangerous Terrain (minefield) in base contact with element.
Performance upgrade allowing movement up to 3x M in a straight line. Only one facing change at beginning or end. Mid-movement turning not allowed except on Paved terrain.
